policy: Major market events that create second-order opportunities not yet priced into Level 2-3 supply chain names. Each event maps to beneficiary tickers that the scanner should weight more heavily when signals converge. Status: anticipated = not yet public / active = unfolding now / watch = monitor for inflection.

events.name: SpaceX / Starlink IPO
events.summary: SpaceX planning Starlink segment IPO, expected 2026-2027. The listing of the world's largest satellite internet constellation will reprice the entire space economy — attention flows to listed adjacencies before and after the filing.
events.investment_implication: Public space economy names re-price BEFORE SpaceX lists. Level 2 plays with real revenue and moats are underappreciated right now. Look for: S-1 filing, SEC registration statement, underwriter announcements.
events.supply_chain_note: RKLB = direct comp + sentiment lift + launch services demand. IRDM = validated LEO model, M&A target. KTOS = ground infrastructure + defense adjacency. ASTS = direct satellite comms play.

events.name: Nuclear SMR Accelerated Permitting
events.summary: NRC is fast-tracking SMR construction permit applications. TerraPower (Natrium), Kairos Power, X-energy, and NuScale all have active applications. AI data centers explicitly requesting nuclear baseload. Multi-year tailwind with compounding permit approvals.
events.investment_implication: Each permit approval is a catalyst for BWXT (component supplier) and LEU (HALEU fuel, required for advanced SMRs). CEG/VST already re-priced. The Level 3 fuel/component layer is still at trough.
events.supply_chain_note: LEU is the only US-licensed HALEU producer — all US-deployed SMRs will need their fuel. BWXT makes reactor components. Each permit is a forward order for these companies.

events.name: Golden Dome Missile Defense Initiative
events.summary: DOD Golden Dome initiative: next-generation missile defense network with AI-enabled targeting, drone interception, and space-based sensors. $20B+ initial appropriation with multiyear expansion. Historically the largest defense modernization since SDI.
events.investment_implication: Autonomous drone and radar systems are the Level 2 play. RTX, LMT, NOC are Level 1 (obvious). KTOS (autonomous drones), RKLB (space-based sensors), LDOS (software integration) are Level 2-3.
events.supply_chain_note: KTOS Valkyrie drone is specifically cited in Golden Dome autonomous intercept scenarios. RKLB has space sensor payload capability. Both are still at cyclical troughs.

events.name: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis
events.summary: Hyperscalers cannot build AI data centers fast enough due to grid power constraints. Microsoft, Google, Amazon all signing direct nuclear PPAs. Power availability is now the binding constraint on AI buildout — not compute or capital.
events.investment_implication: Every MW of new power capacity is now critical. Nuclear (CEG, VST already re-priced), grid equipment (ETN, GEV, HUBB), and independent power producers. NXT (solar tracking) is a Level 3 beneficiary as utility-scale solar is the fastest-to-deploy capacity.
events.supply_chain_note: NXT solar trackers are utility-scale — hyperscalers signing direct solar PPAs use NXT hardware. HUBB electrical equipment goes into every data center substation.

events.name: US-China Semiconductor Export Controls
events.summary: US expanding export restrictions on advanced semiconductor equipment and AI chips to China. AMAT, KLAC, LRCX at risk of further China revenue restrictions. ASML already restricted on EUV. Each new rule creates a revenue headwind for equipment companies.
events.investment_implication: This creates a trough for equipment names (AMAT, KLAC, LRCX) that are fundamentally strong but face near-term China revenue drag. The moat is intact — the trough is policy-created, not structural. Monitor for further rule tightening.
events.supply_chain_note: AMAT gets ~30% revenue from China, KLAC ~35%. Each new BIS rule is an incremental headwind. Equipment names are already partially discounted for this — but watch for escalation.

events.name: Broadcom Custom ASIC Supercycle
events.summary: Google, Meta, Apple, and ByteDance all engaged with AVGO for custom AI ASICs. This is a 2026-2029 revenue cycle unlike anything in AVGO's history. Q2 FY2026 AI revenue $10.8B +143% YoY — the dip after earnings was narrative-driven not fundamental.
events.investment_implication: AVGO post-earnings dip (June 2026) is an active entry window. The -8% gap fill created a buy zone. Level 3 supply chain: ENTG (chip materials), MU (HBM for custom ASICs), ONTO (process control). These see demand pull from every custom ASIC tape-out.
events.supply_chain_note: ENTG supplies advanced materials for every custom ASIC fab process. ONTO does inspection on advanced packaging — every custom chip tape-out uses their tools.
